steps.description: Click the "Platform" dropdown menu in the Samples tab within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension for Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Message Extension (4)" option in the "Platform capability..." dropdown menu within the Samples tab of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Bot" dropdown menu within the "Samples" panel of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code to display agent type filter options.

Click the "Create" button for the "Proactive Messaging" sample in the "Samples" tab of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
And input ${{var:app_name:bot-proactive-messaging-teamsfx}} into the run time variable.
Click the “Sign in to Azure” link under the “ACCOUNTS” section in the MICROSOFT 365 AGENTS TOOLKIT panel of Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Debug" dropdown button in the top-left of the Visual Studio Code Run and Debug panel to select or manage the debug configuration.
Click the "Debug (Chrome)" option in the debug configuration dropdown menu within the Run and Debug panel of Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Debug" dropdown in the Run and Debug panel of Visual Studio Code to select a debug configuration.
Click the "Welcome" card labeled "Resend welcome card of this Bot" in the ProactiveMessagesTeamsFx bot interface within Microsoft Teams (web app).
Click the "Send" button in the message input area of the ProactiveMessagesTeamsFx bot chat within Microsoft Teams (web interface), to submit the message "Resend welcome card of this Bot".
